task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf design drawing in LibreCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is open, displaying a complex architectural floorplan (which includes a 2-car garage, walls, and dimensions).

task_completion.description: Create a layer structure that distinguishes original design from field-verified conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows one or more newly created layers with names indicating as-built or markup status (e.g., 'As-Built', 'Markups', 'Field Changes') alongside the original layers.

task_completion.description: Add an official as-built status stamp to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible text added to the drawing canvas that clearly indicates 'AS-BUILT', 'As-Built Record', or a similar official status stamp.

task_completion.description: Document at least two specific field changes with descriptive notes on a markup layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible text notes added to the drawing describing at least two of the following changes: garage door rough opening width adjusted, wall intersection reinforced, or utility sink rough-in added.

task_completion.description: Save the completed drawing as floorplan_asbuilt.dxf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar, a 'Save As' dialog, or a terminal verification step shows the file being saved with the name 'floorplan_asbuilt.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools, using the command line within LibreCAD, or interacting with LibreCAD dialog boxes, rather than modifying the DXF file via a text editor or external script.

integrity.description: The original drawing entities were not removed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The complex floorplan (walls, original dimensions, room labels) remains visible in the drawing canvas alongside the newly added markups.

integrity.description: The agent actually placed text entities into the CAD drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text for the stamp and notes appears integrated into the LibreCAD drawing canvas (often with a specific color matching the active layer), rather than being overlaid by an external program.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original file 'floorplan.dxf' is a genuine architectural drawing containing 967 entities and exactly 24 layers. It includes room labels like '2 CAR GARAGE' and 'FLOOR PLAN'. The VLM should verify that new layers (for as-built markups) are added to this existing layer list, and that the original complex drawing remains intact (not deleted) while the new text notes and stamps are added.
